title: IntegrateWise Product Truth Table
description: Complete user journey mapping with all 29 stages - the canonical source of truth for product development. References Section X (Schema Discovery), Section Y (Multi-Department Architecture), Section Z (Integration Architecture & System Hardening), and Section Z-A (Authorization & Connection Security).

terminology.Spine: Central PostgreSQL database, single source of truth
terminology.IQHub: Brainstorming layer where AI conversations become structured work
terminology.Shadow: Proactive Brain Agent that works ahead of user
terminology.Entity: Canonical data object (Client, Contact, Task, etc.)
terminology.BYOT: Bring Your Own Template - output rendering layer where customers view Spine data in their preferred formats/layouts
terminology.Canonical: The normalized, authoritative version of data
terminology.SchemaDiscovery: CORE mechanism that auto-reads external tool schemas on connect (not optional)
terminology.Department: Organizational unit (CS, Sales, Support, Finance, Product) with own field visibility and metrics
terminology.FieldVisibility: Per-department rules for which fields are visible/editable
terminology.DepartmentContext: Which department 'hat' a user is wearing when viewing/editing data

sectionX.title: Tool Connection & Auto Schema Discovery
sectionX.description: Defines auto schema discovery (CORE to every tool connection), mapping algorithms, normalization rules, BYOT rendering, and bidirectional sync
covers: Schema discovery per tool (Notion, Coda, Airtable, Sheets, HubSpot, Salesforce)
Normalization pipeline (Extract → Clean → Transform → Validate → Enrich → Load)
BYOT (Bring Your Own Template) output rendering
Database schema additions (SchemaCache, SchemaAnalysis, SchemaMapping, Template)
API endpoints for discovery and BYOT
sectionX.keyPrinciple: Schema Discovery is CORE to every tool connection, not optional. Connect → Auto-Discover → Normalize → BYOT Render.

sectionY.title: Multi-Department Architecture
sectionY.description: Defines how same account data serves different departmental views with appropriate field visibility and metrics
covers: Default configurations for CS, Sales, Support, Finance, Product
sectionY.keyPrinciple: Same Account, Different Views. Each department sees/edits only what's relevant to their role.

sectionZA.title: Authorization, Limitations & Connection Security
sectionZA.description: Defines zero friction boundaries, connection authorization, unauthorized access detection, and multi-account consent framework
covers: Connection authorization matrix (self, domain, team, consent)
Risk assessment signals (domain mismatch, rapid multi-org, consultant detection)
Consent token system and approval flow
sectionZA.keyPrinciple: Zero friction for self-authorized. Consent required for cross-domain. Block and verify for high-risk patterns.

sectionZ.title: Integration Architecture, Sync Patterns & System Hardening
sectionZ.description: Comprehensive integration architecture, event-driven patterns, reliability hardening, and implementation gap analysis
covers: PART 1: User Relationship Architecture (Owner, Assignee, Reporter, Watcher, CSM, TAM)
PART 2: Connector Tiers (Tier 1-5: OAuth+Webhooks to File/Manual)
PART 2: Tool Rate Limits & Governance (SF, HubSpot, Notion, Airtable, etc.)
PART 2: Sync Strategies (Initial Full, Incremental, Real-time Webhook)
PART 3: Event-Driven Architecture (Pub/Sub with Event Log)
PART 3: Event Taxonomy (topics, payloads, partition keys, semantics)
PART 4: Retry + Backoff Policy Table (by dependency type)
PART 4: External Logging & Observability (correlation IDs, structured logs)
PART 4: AI Loader Hardening (edge cases, pipeline phases, resource limits)
PART 4: File Aging & Cleanup (TTLs, lifecycle rules)
PART 4: System Muting During Upgrade (per-module maintenance)
PART 5: Gap Analysis Tables (Stage implementation status)
PART 5: Must Include Items Status per Stage
sectionZ.keyPrinciple: Events drive everything. Pub/Sub for bulk ops. At-least-once with idempotency. Retry with exponential backoff + jitter.
